raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/year> my XXXX XXXX XXXX was charged two times by " XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX one {$1400.00} and the other {$4400.00}. I called Citibank to dispute those charges I didn't place and didn't authorize anyone to place. Citibank offered security credits to me on XX/XX/XXXX. However, I found the {$4400.00} charge was reversed on the XXXX statement. I called Citibank and asked the reason. They told me that the merchant sent them the passport ID of the person and I needed to sign it. I told them that I didn't receive anything to sign by mail. They told me that a notice was sent by email and the document was in my account. It took me a long time to find the document, which I signed, scanned and sent back to Citibank on XXXX XXXX XXXX. Since then the status was " under investigation ''. I called Citibank on XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX. The last time the person I talked called XXXX and she told me the status in still " under investigation '' and it normally took 1 or 2 billing cycles. However, it is more than 3 billing cycles since I found this charge and notified Citibank. The fake passport ID has different gender and age than mine, which should be available to Citibank. I don't know why they reversed the {$4400.00} one, but not the {$1400.00} one and why it took so long without any noticeable progress.
